LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WILLIAM MAYTA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD DISCOVERY OF WRONG DOING WITH MORE PARTICULARITY AND TO PLEAD DEFENDANT'S MISREPRESENTATIONS AND/OR A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONCEALMENT/DECEIT. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS TO SUPPORT ALTER EGO LIABILITY, PLAINTIFF'S CONTRACT WAS WITH THE CORPORATION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WILLIAM MAYTA MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE GRANTED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD A BASIS FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND THERE ARE NO FACTS THAT SHOW THE TORT OF ANOTHER DOCTRINE APPLIES. IF PLAINTIFF CURES THE ERRORS CITED IN THE DEMURRER, THEN PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND 10% INTEREST MAY BE PROPER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DISCOVERY 610, DEFENDANT WILLIAM MAYTA'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO JUDICIAL COUNCIL FORM INTERROGATORIES (NO.2) IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT MUST COMPLY WITHIN 10 DAYS. THE COURT IMPOSES A MONETARY SANCTION OF $1,000 TO DEFENDANT FOR BOTH MOTIONS, PAYABLE TO PLAINTIFF. JUDGE: WALLACE P. DOUGLASS, NOT REPORTED.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-04-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WILLIAM MAYTA DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Margaret M. Farley, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 492-8690 appearing for Defendant and moving party; Jeffrey C. Cannon, Esq. Attorney at Law, (760) 779-5645 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/EHG) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR# 7489

Attorney at Law, (415) 492-8690 appearing for Defendant and moving party; Jeffrey C. Cannon, Esq. Attorney at Law, (760) 779-5645 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: MOTION IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/EHG) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR# 7489

Re Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Judicial Council Form Interrogatories (No.2) & Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances WERE STATED.

After hearing, DEFENDANT WILLIAM MAYTA's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Judicial Council Form Interrogatories (No.2) IS GRANTED. Defendant must comply within 10 days. The court imposes a monetary sanction of $1,000 to defendant for both motions, payable to plaintiff. Judge: Wallace P. Douglass, not reported.

Re Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Judicial Council Form Interrogatories & Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances WERE STATED.

After hearing, DEFENDANT WILLIAM MAYTA's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Judicial Council Form Interrogatories IS GRANTED. Defendant must comply within 10 days. The court imposes a monetary sanction of $1,000 to defendant for both motions, payable to plaintiff. Judge: Wallace P. Douglass, not reported.

Re Notice Motion To Quash For Lack Of Personal Juridiction
Off Calendar Mar-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS DOMINIC BIANCO, ARROWCREEK, LTD., Re Notice of Motion to Quash For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Off Calendar, first amended complaint filed. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WILLIAM MAYTA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend to plead discovery of wrong doing with more particularity and to plead defendant's misrepresentations and/or a cause of action for concealment/deceit. Plaintiff must allege facts to support alter ego liability, plaintiff's contract was with the corporation. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WILLIAM MAYTA MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike granted with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead a basis for attorney fees and there are no facts that show the tort of another doctrine applies. If plaintiff cures the errors cited in the demurrer, then plaintiff's claims for punitive damages and 10% interest may be proper. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
